skill.name: chapter-outline-generator
skill.description: Creates structured book chapter outlines with key scenes, plot progression, character development, themes, and chapter transitions.
skill.summary: Plan book chapters with narrative structure, character arcs, themes, and scene-level direction.
skill.author: Amraha-Anwar

security_audit.summary: The static weak cryptographic algorithm finding at SKILL.md line 3 is a false positive. The line is only a natural-language description of book outlining behavior and contains no cryptographic code, scripts, commands, network access, or prompt injection attempt.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 3, but this line is frontmatter description text for a writing skill. No weak cryptographic algorithm, executable code, or security-sensitive operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line contains only a prose description of chapter outlining. The reviewed file contains no cryptographic functions or executable implementation.

content.value_statement: Writers often lose momentum when a story idea lacks chapter-level structure. This skill turns core story details into a clear outline with scenes, arcs, themes, and transitions.
actual_capabilities: Asks for missing story basics such as title, genre, plot summary, and chapter count.
Organizes chapters around standard narrative stages from setup through resolution.
Defines key events, conflicts, emotional tone, themes, and subplots for each chapter.
Includes character development moments and continuity guidance across the outline.
Formats the result as a Markdown list with clear chapter sub-points.
limitations: Does not write full chapters or polished manuscript prose.
Requires enough story context to create a useful outline.
Does not verify market trends, genre rules, or publishing requirements.
May need revision for complex nonlinear structures or multiple point-of-view timelines.

use_cases.title: Draft a First Novel Outline
use_cases.description: Turn a premise and chapter count into a complete planning document before drafting begins.

use_cases.title: Expand a Series Installment
use_cases.description: Map plot turns, character beats, and chapter transitions for the next book in a series.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Story Workshop Plan
use_cases.description: Create a structured outline for reviewing pacing, theme, conflict, and continuity with collaborators.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Chapter Outline
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you have a simple premise and need a first structure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a chapter outline for a book titled [title]. Genre: [genre]. Main plot: [plot summary]. Number of chapters: [number].

prompt_templates.title: Character-Focused Outline
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when character growth is the main planning priority.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a chapter outline for [title] with [number] chapters. Include key events, conflicts, and a clear emotional arc for [main character].

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Plot Outline
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when the story has subplots, lore, or several major threads.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a detailed chapter outline for [title]. Track the main plot, subplots, foreshadowing, lore, themes, and transitions between chapters.

prompt_templates.title: Revision Outline Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you already have a rough outline and need stronger structure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this rough chapter plan and rebuild it into a stronger outline with setup, inciting incident, rising action, midpoint, climax, falling action, and resolution: [outline].

output_examples.input: A 12-chapter fantasy novel about an apprentice mapmaker who discovers a hidden kingdom.
output_examples.output: A Markdown chapter list with setup, discovery, rising threats, midpoint reversal, climax, resolution, character growth, themes, and chapter transitions.

output_examples.input: A contemporary romance with 18 chapters, dual protagonists, and a workplace conflict.
output_examples.output: A chapter-by-chapter romance outline that tracks relationship tension, external conflict, emotional beats, subplots, and turning points.

output_examples.input: A mystery outline that feels slow in the middle.
output_examples.output: A revised structure with stronger clues, escalating suspects, midpoint pressure, chapter hooks, and a clearer path to the reveal.

best_practices: Provide the genre, core conflict, main characters, and desired chapter count before asking for the outline.
Share known themes, subplots, and point-of-view rules so the outline can maintain continuity.
Review the result for pacing and adjust chapter counts before drafting scenes.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a complete book when you only need a planning outline.
Do not omit the main plot summary and expect detailed chapter progression.
Do not treat the first outline as final without checking genre fit and continuity.

faq.question: Can this skill write full chapters?
faq.answer: No. It is designed to create chapter outlines, not finished manuscript prose.

faq.question: What information should I provide first?
faq.answer: Provide the title, genre, main plot summary, and target number of chapters.

faq.question: Can it support nonfiction books?
faq.answer: It is written for book outlines and story structure. It may need adaptation for nonfiction chapter planning.

faq.question: Does it handle character arcs?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks for and includes character development moments across chapters.

faq.question: What format does it produce?
faq.answer: It produces a Markdown list with chapter titles and clear sub-points.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, or Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
